action.reasoning: Observation: Test EER got worse (0.2745 vs 0.0956 baseline). Best.pt was overwritten by the latest run, which selected the epoch-0 checkpoint based on dev_eer=0.0006. But epoch 0 with low dev EER doesn't generalize. The earlier successful iter 6 trained 3 epochs and used the FINAL model (last.pt-equivalent). Analysis: Selecting by dev EER picks the epoch-0 model that overfits quickly on the in-domain dev. The earlier run selected end-of-training (which has had 3 full epochs of codec aug). Also predict.py uses max_sec=6.0 but train uses 4.0 — should be consistent. Decision: Retrain 3 epochs with the same setup but save LAST checkpoint as best (not the dev-EER selected one), since dev is in-domain and unreliable for generalization. Use last.pt for eval submission.
